name: codebase_analysis
description: Performs comprehensive static analysis of a codebase using scc, returning strictly typed metrics — language distribution, complexity profiles, COCOMO estimates, and per-file breakdowns. Agents reason over structural health, not raw directory listings.

primary.note: Boyter's scc (Sloc Cloc and Code). Go-native. Provides complexity, COCOMO, and JSON output.

fallback.note: Rust-native line counter. Reduced fidelity — no complexity, no COCOMO, no per-file granularity in normalization. Language totals only.

granularity.description: Output depth. 'summary' returns totals only. 'per_language' returns language breakdowns. 'per_file' returns file-level detail nested under each language. Token cost scales with granularity.

exclude_dirs.description: Directory names to exclude from traversal (e.g., 'node_modules', 'target', 'dist'). Passed to scc --exclude-dir.

exclude_patterns.description: Regex patterns for file exclusion. Passed to scc --not-match. Use for fine-grained file filtering beyond directory exclusion.

languages.description: Restrict analysis to specific languages (e.g., 'Go', 'Rust', 'Python', 'TypeScript'). Passed to scc --include-lang. Empty array analyses all detected languages.

sort_by.description: Sort language breakdowns by the given metric. 'code' is the default to surface primary implementation volume.

complexity.description: Enable cyclomatic complexity estimation. scc calculates this by default; set false to disable for faster execution on massive codebases.

cocomo.description: Include COCOMO 81 cost estimation (effort in person-months, team size, schedule months, cost). Adds ~5% token overhead.

cocomo_project_type.description: COCOMO project type. 'organic' for small teams/good experience, 'semi-detached' for mixed, 'embedded' for tight constraints.

min_code_lines.description: Minimum lines of code for a file to be included in counts. Filters out trivial files (e.g., 1-line __init__.py markers).

max_files_per_language.description: Hard cap on per-file entries returned per language when granularity is 'per_file'. Enforced in normalisation to prevent token saturation in large monorepos.

include_binary.description: Include binary files in file counts. Does not attempt to count lines in binaries.

no_ignore.description: Bypass .gitignore, .ignore, and ignore patterns. Analyse the full directory tree.

follow_symlinks.description: Follow symbolic links during directory traversal.

total_files.description: Total files analysed across all languages.

total_lines.description: Sum of code + comment + blank lines across all files.

complexity_score.description: Aggregate cyclomatic complexity across all files. Null if complexity was disabled or fallback was used.

estimated_languages.description: Number of distinct programming languages detected.

cocomo.description: Null if cocomo was disabled, unsupported by fallback, or no code was found.

cost_usd.description: Estimated cost in USD at default salary multiplier.

truncated.description: True if per-file detail was capped by max_files_per_language before traversal completed.

used_fallback.description: True if tokei fallback was invoked instead of scc.

name.description: Language name as detected by scc (e.g., 'Go', 'Python', 'TypeScript').

complexity.description: Per-language cyclomatic complexity. Null if disabled or unavailable.

code_percentage.description: Percentage of total codebase code lines attributed to this language.

files_detail.description: Omitted entirely from JSON payload if granularity is not 'per_file', to preserve token density.

warnings.description: Non-fatal advisories (e.g., binary files encountered, symlink loops avoided, inaccurate language detection).

used_fallback.description: True if tokei fallback was attempted and also failed.

args: {% for dir in exclude_dirs %}--exclude-dir={{ dir }}{% endfor %}
{% for pat in exclude_patterns %}--not-match={{ pat }}{% endfor %}
{% for lang in languages %}--include-lang={{ lang }}{% endfor %}
execution.note: scc --format json emits a JSON array of language objects. --by-file nests a Files array inside each language. --cocomo adds COCOMO fields per language. scc exits 0 on success (including empty results) and non-zero on actual errors.

pipeline.description: Parse scc JSON array output. Each element is a language block with Name, Bytes, Code, Comment, Blank, Complexity, Count, and optionally Files and Cocomo fields.

pipeline.condition: parsed array is empty OR path contains no recognised files

warnings: No recognised source files found in path.

pipeline.description: Empty codebase is not an error. Emit valid zeroed envelope. Skip remaining pipeline steps.

transforms.null_if_not_per_file: If granularity != 'per_file', set files_detail to null so the field is stripped during assembly. If granularity == 'per_file' but Files array is null (scc anomaly), emit empty array.

pipeline.description: Map each scc language object to the transcend output schema. Compute lines as the sum of code, comment, and blank.

pipeline.action: Iterate all languages.files_detail. Convert all 'path' strings to strictly normalized POSIX relative paths (e.g., stripping redundant './' prefixes).
pipeline.description: Ensures path formatting is identical to downstream tools (like rg or sd) so chaining operations do not fail on path mismatches.
Roll up per-language metrics into top-level totals. complexity_score is null if any language lacks complexity (fallback or disabled).

pipeline.action: For each language, code_percentage = (language.code / code_lines) * 100. Round to 1 decimal place. If code_lines is 0, set 0.0.
pipeline.description: Provide relative language dominance for agent reasoning about tech-stack composition.

pipeline.condition: cocomo input was true AND scc output contains Cocomo fields

mapping.schedule_months: Cocomo.Months | max across languages (scc reports per-language; take longest schedule)

pipeline.description: Aggregate COCOMO estimates across all languages. scc emits per-language COCOMO; we surface project-level totals. If fields are absent, cocomo is set to null.

pipeline.action: For each language, slice files_detail to max_files_per_language entries. Sort order is preserved from scc (deterministic if sort_by is not 'none').
pipeline.description: Enforce per-language file cap to prevent token saturation in monorepos with thousands of files per language.

pipeline.condition: truncated not already set AND granularity == 'per_file'
pipeline.description: Explicitly set truncated to false when no capping occurred.

pipeline.action: Remove files_detail key entirely from any language object where it is null. Remove complexity key from any language where it is null. Remove top-level cocomo key if null. Remove complexity_score if null.
pipeline.description: Token-density optimisation: omit null-valued fields rather than emitting null placeholders.
Merge all computed fields into the final success envelope. Set used_fallback to false. Set execution_ms from runtime timer.

args: {% if sort_by == 'code' %}--sort=code{% elif sort_by == 'comments' %}--sort=comments{% elif sort_by == 'blanks' %}--sort=blanks{% elif sort_by == 'lines' %}--sort=lines{% endif %}
output_gaps: complexity is unavailable on all fields (set to null and stripped)
cocomo is unavailable (set to null and stripped)
per-file granularity is unavailable; languages array contains totals only
exclude_dirs, exclude_patterns, languages inputs have no effect on tokei CLI; tokei analyses all files it recognises
include_binary, no_ignore, follow_symlinks inputs have no effect
min_code_lines has no effect; tokei counts all files
code_percentage is computed from tokei totals but may differ from scc due to different language detection heuristics

compatible_downstream.description: Scope search to specific languages by passing language names as file_types, or search within the analysed path.
View high-complexity or high-line-count files identified during analysis.
Run AST-level queries against files flagged by analysis (e.g., largest files, highest complexity).
Target refactoring operations against specific file sets identified by language or size metrics.
Read full file contents of files surfaced in the per-file breakdown.

compatible_upstream.description: Receive a directory path from fd or eza to scope analysis.
Receive a specific path or subset from fd for targeted analysis.
Analyse a codebase after initial discovery via search (e.g., find all services, then analyse their directories).

vanquish.note: Language names and file paths carry structural signal for Vanquish embedding. Numeric metrics (lines, complexity) are not semantically embeddable and should be stored as metadata only.

performance.note: Token pressure scales linearly with granularity. 'summary' produces ~50 tokens. 'per_language' produces ~20 tokens per language. 'per_file' produces ~15 tokens per file. Use max_files_per_language aggressively in monorepos.
